column1: # drain-mind tell lexicon — single source of truth for insight-bearing session phrases.
#   pattern     — the tell phrase; interpreted case-insensitively.
#   category    — one of the valid categories below.
#   self-critique       lesson/mistake language; insight about what went wrong.
#   trust-rupture       user pushback or expressed doubt.
#   failure-report      "didn't fire / hasn't moved / never registered" tense family.
#   state-collision     stale context, dirty state, conflicting data.
#   filing-decision     explicit decision to file, supersede, or close an issue.
#   deferral            explicit deferral of work or decision.
#   self-correction     assistant catching itself mid-task ("I'm thinking about
#                       this wrong" / "that didn't go right"); assistant-turn only,
#   - All patterns are matched CASE-INSENSITIVELY against transcript text.
#   - literal rows: matched as plain substrings (grep -Fi).
#   - regex  rows: matched as extended regexes (grep -Ei); every regex in this file
# This file is consumed by the (future) tell-scanner (#371).
# Referenced by: claude/commands/drain-mind.md § Step 3 extraction tells.
# Validated by: make validate-lexicon (wired into CI `checks` job).
# --- friction-slug (near-zero false-positive; exact category slug names) ---
licensing is too expensive|path .* that doesn'?t require (it|the api)
disciplined about .* measur|before and after (changes|each change)
does not exist in the project
should( actually)? be an issue|should be on the board
don'?t want to (set|arm|enable) .* until|confident it'?s working
